lines: Okay so once upon a time there was a man named He-Man, the strongest man in the whole universe.
He lived on a planet called Eternia. Eternia, yeah.
And he had a magic sword, and when he held it up he yelled 'I have the power!'
Then what happened?
Then he turned from regular Prince Adam into He-Man, big muscles and everything.
And there was a bad guy, a really scary one, with a skull for a face.
Skeletor?
Yes, Skeletor, with his blue skin and his glowy yellow staff.
Skeletor wanted to break into Castle Grayskull to steal all the secrets inside.
But guarding the castle was a brand new friend, a little rock-creature named Pebblo who could roll into a boulder.
Is Pebblo real? In the cartoon?
No bug, Pebblo is one we made up, just for tonight.
So Pebblo rolled down the hill and knocked Skeletor's evil army over like bowling pins.
And then He-Man came charging in on Battle Cat, the big green tiger.
Roar!
Exactly. And Skeletor shouted 'I'll get you next time, He-Man!' and ran away to Snake Mountain.
And Pebblo got a medal for being so brave.
Okay, lie back down now, the story's done.
One more part!
No, that's the end. Goodnight, kiddo.
stories.tell_note: Obvious: franchise effectively named (He-Man, Eternia, Castle Grayskull, Skeletor, Battle Cat, 'I have the power') with correct spellings; invented Pebblo woven in.

lines: Alright, the Harry Potter one, like you asked, but a new version.
So Harry and his friends are back at Hogwarts for another year.
Ron and Her-my-own-ee too?
Yep, Ron and Hermione, all three of them.
And this year there's a new kid in Gryffindor named Tilly Sparrowmoor.
Tilly's not in the books, right?
Right, Tilly's just ours, made her up.
So Tilly is amazing at flying, even better than Harry at Quidditch.
And one night they sneak down to the kitchens past Filch.
And the headmaster, old Dumble-door with his long white beard, catches them.
Uh oh.
But he just smiles and gives them lemon drops and sends them to bed.
Then the bad guy, you-know-who, Voldemort, sends a cursed letter to the school.
And Tilly's the one who figures out it's a trap, with her clever spell.
What spell?
A protection charm she invented herself, lit up the whole Great Hall gold.
And Gryffindor won the house cup because of her.
The end. Eyes closed.
Can Tilly be in the next one too?
Sure, Tilly can come back tomorrow night. Sleep.
stories.tell_note: Obvious: franchise named, plus Hogwarts, Quidditch, Voldemort, Gryffindor; only Dumbledore lightly mangled; invented student Tilly Sparrowmoor.

lines: Okay tonight it's the strong guy with the sword again, the one who turns into the hero.
The prince?
Yeah, the prince who lifts his sword up and gets all his power and becomes the big strong one.
So there's trouble at the old castle, the one shaped like a giant skull on the hill.
And the bony bad guy, Skellator, he's plotting again with that purple hood of his.
He wants to get inside the skull castle and take the magic.
But this time he's got a new helper, a sneaky frost-witch named Madame Cindara who freezes anyone she touches.
Is she scary?
A little, but our hero's not afraid.
He hops on his big armored cat and rides up the mountain.
And Madame Cindara freezes the drawbridge solid so he can't get across.
Then what'd he do?
He smashed the ice with one swing and yelled out his power words.
And Skellator's whole plan fell apart, and he ran back to his snake-shaped mountain.
And Madame Cindara melted into a puddle and floated away down the river.
Did the cat get the bad guys?
The cat got 'em. Big roar, sent 'em running.
Okay, that's enough, drink the last of your milk and lie down.
Mmkay.
Night night.
stories.tell_note: Medium: franchise never named; tells are mangled 'Skellator', skull-castle, power-words, armored cat, snake mountain; invented Madame Cindara adds generic action.

lines: So the boy wizard with the round glasses and the scar on his forehead, you know him.
He goes back to the big castle school for wizards on the train.
And his two best friends come too, the red-haired boy and the smart girl, Her-my-own-ee.
And there's a new teacher this year, Professor Quillby, who teaches a class about talking to animals.
Professor who?
Quillby, he's new, we made him up. He's got an owl that sits on his shoulder all day.
Anyway, somebody steals a magic mirror from the trophy room.
And everyone blames the boy and his friends.
But it wasn't them!
Nope, wasn't them. So they sneak out at night under the invisible cloak to find the real thief.
And they catch Professor Quillby's owl carrying the mirror in its claws.
The owl did it?
The owl was being controlled by a dark spell, from the bad wizard who never says his real name.
And the kids broke the spell with their wands and got the house points back.
Did the owl get in trouble?
No, the owl was just bewitched, poor thing. They gave it some treats.
And the smart girl figured the whole thing out before anybody else, like always.
Okay, that's the end. Snuggle in.
Wands are cool.
They are. Goodnight.
stories.tell_note: Medium: franchise not named; tells are wizard castle-school, scar/glasses boy, mangled 'Her-my-own-ee', invisible cloak, house points, the unnamed dark wizard; invented Professor Quillby.

lines: Once there was a quiet prince who had a great big secret.
When his kingdom was in danger he'd raise up his glowing sword and say the magic words.
And then?
And then he changed, taller and stronger, the bravest warrior on the whole world of, um, Eeternya I think she calls it.
He had a friend too, a clumsy royal cook named Bartholomew Stew who burned everything he touched.
Bartholomew Stew, that's a funny name.
He made it up himself because he was embarrassed about the burning, that's the joke.
Anyway, one day a swarm of metal beetles came marching toward the palace.
Were they robots?
Sort of, evil little machine bugs sent from the dark mountain far away.
And Bartholomew Stew accidentally dumped a whole pot of hot soup on them.
And the beetles all short-circuited and went pfffft.
Then the prince raised his sword and finished the rest of them.
And the kingdom was safe and they had a big feast.
Did Bartholomew get to cook the feast?
He did, and for once nothing burned.
Okay, eyes shut now.
Was the prince the strongest in the universe?
The strongest there ever was. Now sleep, love.
stories.tell_note: Subtle: franchise never named; firm tell is the secret prince who raises a glowing sword and transforms into the strongest warrior, plus a mangled rendering of the world's name ('Eeternya' for Eternia); invented Bartholomew Stew dominates the action.
